name: Zulip MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Zulip — the open-source team messaging platform with threaded conversations. Enables AI agents to send messages to Zulip streams and topics, read conversations, search message history, and manage Zulip workspace interactions — supporting AI-driven team communication workflows.
use_cases: Sending updates and notifications to Zulip streams from AI workflow automation agents
Reading and searching Zulip conversation history from AI research and knowledge agents
Monitoring Zulip streams for specific topics or mentions from AI assistant agents
Integrating Zulip team communication into AI-driven project management workflows
not_for: Teams without Zulip workspace (self-hosted or Zulip Cloud)
Real-time presence or video meeting features (Zulip is text messaging)
best_when: A team using Zulip for asynchronous communication wants AI agents to post updates, search conversations, and integrate Zulip messaging into automated workflows.
avoid_when: You use Slack, Teams, or Discord. Zulip has a smaller user base but strong open-source and academic community following.

auth.notes: Zulip API key + email required. Bot accounts recommended for agent access. API key from Zulip account settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Zulip Cloud free: unlimited users, 5GB storage; Zulip self-hosted is free
pricing.paid_tiers: Zulip Cloud Standard/Plus for more storage and features
pricing.notes: Zulip is free open source. Cloud plans available. MCP server is free open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Zulip's stream+topic model differs from Slack channels — agents must specify both stream and topic
Use bot accounts (not personal API keys) for agent access to avoid rate limit issues
Message editing and deletion policies vary by organization — check Zulip settings
Community MCP — may not support all Zulip API endpoints (webhooks, custom emoji, etc.)
agent_readiness.security_notes: Team communications may be confidential. API key required. Use bot account with minimal permissions. HTTPS. Zulip supports SOC2.

security_score_components.security_notes: Team chat data may be confidential. API key with bot account. HTTPS. SOC2.
